frameworks.name: predictive coding (next-frame prediction)
frameworks.one_line: perception adapts to optimally predict the next expected sensory input

frameworks.name: efficient sensory representation
frameworks.one_line: adaptation reallocates encoding accuracy toward expected future stimuli

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network (PredNet)
frameworks.one_line: RNN trained on naturalistic video as a model of predictive adaptation

frameworks.name: natural input statistics
frameworks.one_line: retinal orientation statistics of freely behaving humans motivate the prediction

core_question: Does perceptual adaptation reflect optimal preparation of sensory encoding for the next expected input?
key_assumptions: adaptation reshapes encoding accuracy across orientation
natural input statistics constrain what future inputs are expected
a future-prediction objective alone can produce adaptation-like effects
manipulations: stability and orientation of recent stimulus history
adaptor orientation in psychophysics and in model input
PredNet vs human as substrate for the same statistics
key_findings: after stable visual input, next-frame local-orientation distribution peaks at the recent mean
PredNet shows increased encoding accuracy at the adaptor orientation, matching humans
this gain emerges without any local adaptation mechanism in the network
interpretations: adaptation optimally prepares sensory representations for the next expected input
increased encoding accuracy at the adaptor follows from a future-prediction objective plus stimulus history
connecting_signals: recurrent neural network model of perception
